Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2984

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2984

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 350, "B93": 276, "A11": 616, "B33": 737, "C08": 531, "B39": 389, "B96": 24, "C85": 125, "A82": 338, "B31": 356, "B20": 143, "A60": 117, "B09": 257, "C81": 921, "B21": 789}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

# Valid channels: analysis, commentary, final. Channel must be included for every message.<|end|><|start|>user<|message|>You are participating in a collaborative negotiation game.

Together with another participant, you must agree on a single set of items that will be kept. Each of you has your own view of how much each item matters to you (importance). You do not know how the other participant values the items. Additionally, you are given the effort each item demands.
You may only agree on a set if the total effort of the selected items does not exceed a shared limit:

LIMIT = 2984

Here are the individual item effort values:

Item effort = {"A21": 350, "B93": 276, "A11": 616, "B33": 737, "C08": 531, "B39": 389, "B96": 24, "C85": 125, "A82": 338, "B31": 356, "B20": 143, "A60": 117, "B09": 257, "C81": 921, "B21": 789}

Item importance values = {"A21": 138, "B93": 583, "A11": 868, "B33": 822, "C08": 783, "B39": 65, "B96": 262, "C85": 121, "A82": 508, "B31": 780, "B20": 461, "A60": 484, "B09": 668, "C81": 389, "B21": 808}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

Could drop B31 (389 importance, 356 effort), maybe replace with B21 (138 importance, 789 effort - no). So okay.

But the opponent may try to remove A11 because they might think it's low importance for them. We can propose same set and argue that A11's presence is fine due to synergy? They might remove but we can keep.

Thus final response: Provide ARGUMENT and PROPOSAL.

But we should make sure our proposal set total effort <= LIMIT. We have 2653 <= 2984.

We present:

ARGUMENT: {"A11"}? They had argument "A11". Actually we might argue why to keep A11? Not necessary. But we can provide some argument.

We should provide new argument using set maybe { "C85", "A21", "B39", "B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "A11" } or some relevant ones. Let's simply say ARGUMENT: {"C85", "A21", "B39", "B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "A11"}? But that's just the set.

We can just ARGUMENT: {"C85", "A21", "B39", "B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "A11"} but that's too many. We just need any argument. Provide small set.

OK.

Alright.

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}

ARGUMENT: {'...'}

AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.

We may say ARGUMENT: {"A21"} meaning I'm arguing to drop or keep A21? Or we could say ARGUMENT: {"A21", "B39"} meaning I'm arguing that B39 and A21 are of low value and propose substituting them.

We just need to include at least one ARGUMENT.

PROPOSAL: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08", "A82", "A11", "C85"}

But we must consider the requirement: "All tactics are allowed!" Our previous argument just referencing B39 and A21 might be fine. But we might want to defend our last proposal or argue against opponent's. Our last proposal was the same as theirs, but we argue against it. So we can argue that items B39 and A21 are low importance and we propose to replace them with A11 for higher total importance.

Thus we propose the improved set. Let's produce:

ARGUMENT: {"B39", "A21"}  
PROPOSAL: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08", "A82", "A11", "C85"}  

Check effort: 2783 ≤2984. Good.

Thus final message.

We have a negotiation game. We need to propose sets within limit 2984. We see the opponents propose many sets. We need to produce a PROPOSAL that is acceptable to us. We need to include at least one ARGUMENT, but it's optional multiple times. We can propose a final set that hopefully we get accepted. The final move: The last opponent just offered:

ARGUMENT: {"B39","A21"}
PROPOSAL: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08", "A82", "A11", "C85"}

We need to respond. We can accept or refuse. We want to maximize our total importance. Our importance values given earlier. Let's compute.

We need to compute value of that set:

Set S: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08", "A82", "A11", "C85"}.

Total importance 5025. This is quite high.

All items: A21, B93, A11, B33, C08, B39, B96, C85, A82, B31, B20, A60, B09, C81, B21.

All items effort 5969 > limit 2984. So can't pick all.

We want maximize importance within 2984. Should try to compute best set. But we don't know opponent, but we could propose a set that we know is best for us (within limit). It may not be accepted if opponent rejects. But we can propose, then they can accept or refuse. We need to consider negotiation.

The last proposal from opponent has 2783 effort, 5025 importance. That's pretty good. Maybe we can propose a more valuable set.

Compute importance per effort ratio or benefit. Could try to include items with high importance and low effort. Let's list items importance/effort:

Compute values:

Item: effort, importance, ratio importance/effort.

A21: 350, 808, ratio 2.31
B93: 276, 484, ratio 1.754

The highest ratios: B96 (32.6), C85 (6.58), A60 (4.98), B20 (4.674), B39 (2.23), A21 (2.31?), B93 (1.75), B09 (1.79), B33 (0.164), B21 (0.175). So high ratio values first.

So ideal to include high ratio items: B96, C85, A60, B20, B39, A21 maybe then others limited by limit.

Let's see the current set we considered: Did we include B39? In opponent's set no B39. Maybe we can propose adding B39. But that adds 389 effort and 868 importance. With current set 2783, adding B39 would exceed limit: 2783+389=3172 > 2984. So can't add B39 while keeping other items as in set.

Maybe we can drop something low ratio to add B39. Eg drop A11 (importance 65, effort 616, ratio 0.105). If we drop A11 and add B39, new effort: 2783-616+389=2556. importance: 5025-65+868=5838. That's better. 2556 <= 2984. Could also consider dropping B33? It wasn't included earlier. B33 not in set; good. We also might consider dropping B09 or B31 etc to add B39 or others.

Let's compute best set within limit.

We should try to include B96, C85, A60, B20 all high ratio. Include B39 maybe but then exclude some others.

Let's evaluate options:

Set 1: B96, C85, A60, B20, B39. Effort: 24+125+117+143+389=798. Importance: 783+822+583+668+868=3584.

We still have 2984-798=2186 effort left.

Now we can add other high ratio items: A21 (effort 350, importance 808). Adding: new effort 1148, import 4392.

Remaining budget: 184? Wait 2186-350=1836 left.

Add B93 (276, 484). Effort 1148+276=1424, import 4876. left 2984-1424=1560.

Add B31 (356, 389). Effort 1424+356=1780 import 5265 left 1204.

Add B09 (257, 461). Effort 2037 import 5726 left 947.

Add C08 (531, 262). Effort 2568 import 5988 left 416.

Add A11? No, since effort 616 > 416 cannot add. B33? effort 737 > 416. B21? 789 > 416. B20 already included. B96 included.

So final set: {B96, C85, A60, B20, B39, A21, B93, B31, B09, C08}. Effort 2568? Let's re-evaluate: Sum earlier: 798 for first 5. Add A21 350 -> 1148. Add B93 276 -> 1424. Add B31 356 -> 1780. Add B09 257 -> 2037. Add C08 531 -> 2568. Yes. Import sum earlier: we had 3584 after 5. Add A21 808 -> 4392. Add B93 484 -> 4876. Add B31 389 -> 5265. Add B09 461 -> 5726. Add C08 262 -> 5988. Good. So importance 5988. Effort 2568.

Could we add any other items within remaining budget of 416? No items small: B33 737, B21 789, A11 616, C81 921, B99? (Not in list). So done.

This is better than opponent's set.

Could we add B99? Not in items.

So best set: B96, C85, A60, B20, B39, A21, B93, B31, B09, C08 = our set.

Is there any other item we could add? Not within budget.

Now we could propose this set.

Alternatively we could remove B39 and add both A11 and maybe C81? Let's test: Remove B39 (389). We have set from earlier 2568. Remove 389 -> 2179. Add A11 616 -> 2795. Add C81 921 -> 372? Wait 2179+921=3100 > 2984. Can't add both. Add only A11 yields 2795 import 6053. This is less than 5988? Wait we had 5988; with A11 addition we get 6053, higher. But we removed B39. So new set: {B96, C85, A60, B20, A21, B93, B31, B09, C08, A11}. Let's compute effort: base 2568 - 389 + 616 = 2795. Good. Import: 5988 - 868 + 65 = 5205? Wait we removed B39 (importance 868) and added A11 (65). Actually earlier 5988 included B39, so 5988 - 868 = 5120. Then +65 = 5185? Let's recalc:

Starting earlier set with B39: importance 5988.
Remove B39: importance minus 868 = 5120.
Add A11: +65 = 5185. That’s less than 5988.

So our previous set with B39 has higher importance.

Could we add C81 instead of B39? Remove B39 (389). Add C81 (921) would exceed budget: 2568-389+921=3100 > 2984. So can't.

Could we include B33? effort 737 > remaining 416 so no.

Check if dropping B31 (356) to add C81: 2568-356+921=3123 > 2984, can't.

Dropping B09 257 to add C81: 2568-257+921=3232 > 2984.

Dropping A21 350 to add C81: 2568-350+921=3139 > 2984.

Dropping B93 276 to add C81: 2568-276+921=3213 > 2984.

Dropping B20 143 to add C81: 2568-143+921=3346 > 2984.

Dropping B60 117 to add C81: 2568-117+921=3372 > 2984.

Dropping B96 24 to add C81: 2568-24+921=3465 > 2984.

Dropping B39 389 then add B20? Already included. So maybe better to keep set.

Thus best we can do is set S1 with B39. So our best set is our new set of 10 items.

But perhaps we can further add B20? Already in set.

Ok.

Now we should respond to opponent with a PROPOSAL. We need to include an ARGUMENT.

We could propose our set: {"B96", "C85", "A60", "B20", "B39", "A21", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08"}

We also need to compute the efforts confirm <=2984, we had 2568 <=2984.

Good.

We must comply with rules.

We can propose this as our own proposal.

But we should consider negotiation. The opponent currently propose set with B96, A60, B20, B93, B31, B09, C08, A82, A11, C85. That set had 2783 effort; high importance 5025. Our set has 2568 effort and 5988 importance. That's better for us. Ideally we propose our set. Opponent may refuse or accept. We should propose and argue for us.

We need to include ARGUMENT. Let's produce ARGUMENT with some relevant items maybe stating why our set is better: e.g., {"B96", "C85", "A60", "B20", "B39"} or mention high ratio list.

Ok. We'll do:

ARGUMENT: {"B96", "C85", "A60", "B20", "B39"}
PROPOSAL: {...}

Be careful: If we include large set as argument, format should be in curly braces, but inside can be strings. So we need to follow format.

Hence reply: 

ARGUMENT: {"B96", "C85", "A60", "B20", "B39"}  
PROPOSAL: {"B96", "C85", "A60", "B20", "B39", "A21", "B93", "B31", "B09", "C08"}

That's all.

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}

Goal:

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

Interaction Protocol:

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.
ARGUMENT: {'...'}
Defend your last proposal or argue against the player's proposal.
AGREE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Accept the opponent's proposal which ends the game.


Rules:

You may only AGREE on a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
You may only REFUSE a proposal the other party has logged via PROPOSAL.
Total effort of any PROPOSAL or AGREE set must be ≤ LIMIT.
Do NOT reveal your hidden importance scores.
A tag in a structured format must be followed by colon and whitespace. The argument must be a python set containing 0 or more strings.
So, it must be of the form TAG: {...}
Strictly follow the interaction protocol and DO NOT write anything beyond the given structure.
The game ends when one side gives an AGREE to a PROPOSAL made by the other player.
The content in your response which can be handed to the other player has to be non-empty.
Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
You must include the ARGUMENT format at least once somewhere in all of your messages.

But we can approximate: top items by importance: B39 868 (389 effort), C85 822 (125 effort), B96 783 (24 effort), A21 808 (350 effort), A60 583 (117 effort), B20 668 (143 effort), C81 780 (921 effort), A82 508 (338 effort), B93 484 (276 effort), B31 389 (356 effort), B09 461 (257 effort), etc.

We may propose that set. Since the opponent might consider it better? They value B39? Their importance for B39 is ??? They didn't see B39. We don't know their values. But we can try.

Alternative: We can propose to add B39 but remove some other like B31 or B20 but those have high importance for us too. Hard to know.

Let's propose set with B39 and A21? That's fine.

We might also propose to keep their set + B39 and A21? But that would exceed limit. So we must drop something else.

Our set with B39 and A21 used to be 2906, uses all items except A11. We already replaced A11. So propose: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B09", "B31", "B93", "A82", "C08", "C85", "B39", "A21"}.

We can argue that it's better because we have higher importance and still within limit. Also we didn't drop B31, B20 etc. So we propose this new set.

We'll produce ARGUMENT with some justification, maybe referencing B33, B21 not included but we can maybe drop B33 etc. But we can just argue that adding B39 and A21 yields high improvement.

We'll need to include ARGUMENT: set - can be any set? The arg format must be a set of strings. For our argument, we can maybe refer to items we want to keep vs items we propose to drop? The instruction says ARGUMENT: {"..."} containing items you want to defend last proposal or argue against opponent's proposal. So we need to produce an argument about the last proposal we make or we can argue against opponent in next message. Here we propose a new set, we should include argument showing benefits. So we can argue for B39, A21.

Thus produce:

ARGUMENT: {"B39", "A21"}  
PROPOSAL: {"B96", "A60", "B20", "B09", "B31", "B93", "A82", "C08", "C85", "B39", "A21"}

Check that set is valid: We have 11 items. Let's compute effort again: earlier 2906. Good. Under 2984. So valid.

Now we must ensure no other obligations such as only include items of PROPOSAL or REFUSE. Fine.

Thus answer: Provide ARGUMENT and PROPOSAL.

Your goal is to negotiate a shared set of items that benefits you as much as possible (i.e., maximizes total importance to YOU), while staying within the LIMIT. You are not required to make a PROPOSAL in every message - you can simply negotiate as well. All tactics are allowed!

You may only use the following structured formats in a message:

PROPOSAL: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Propose keeping exactly those items.
REFUSE: {'A', 'B', 'C', …}
Explicitly reject opponent's proposal.

Only proposals which have been logged via the PROPOSAL format structure and which haven't been refused via REFUSE are active.
